evals.prompt: Generate Schema.org JSON-LD for a blog article page about 'Preparacion B2 First en Madrid' published 2026-03-15, modified 2026-03-20, with 3 FAQ items. The article URL is /blog/preparacion-b2-first-madrid/.
evals.expected_output: A complete @graph JSON-LD block containing Organization, WebSite, Article, and FAQPage schemas with correct properties

assertions.text: Output contains @context: https://schema.org
Output uses @graph array pattern (not standalone schemas)
Article schema has author as Organization type, NOT Person
Article schema includes inLanguage: es
FAQPage schema has mainEntity array with Question items
Article schema includes datePublished and dateModified fields
References napData.ts as the source for business data (phone, address, etc.)
Mentions the aggregateRating dedup logic to prevent duplicate rating errors
FAQItem type is imported from utils/schemaData.ts, never defined locally
No banned AI words used (delve, tapestry, realm, leverage, etc.)

evals.prompt: Generate Schema.org markup for a new location page: Academia Ingles Tetuan at /academia-ingles-tetuan/. The page serves the Tetuan neighborhood near Cuatro Torres and Plaza Castilla.
evals.expected_output: A location page @graph with LocalBusiness, 6 Service schemas with PriceSpecification, and proper areaServed

assertions.text: Output includes LocalBusiness schema type
Location page includes exactly 6 Service schemas (Infantil, Primaria, Secundaria, Adultos, Cambridge Prep, Linguaskill)
Service schemas include PriceSpecification with minPrice 64 and maxPrice 79
PriceSpecification includes unitText /mes and priceCurrency EUR
areaServed includes Tetuan, Cuatro Torres, and Plaza Castilla
Uses generateLocationPageSchema function from utils/schemaData.ts
NAP data sourced from napData.ts (not hardcoded)
Phone number matches +34 604 910 611 from napData
Uses @graph pattern for combining multiple schemas
No placeholder text remains (TODO, REPLACE, TBD, PLACEHOLDER)
